rationale: The equality predicate det_send_single_cmessage_equal requires concrete equality of HashMap fields (post1_self_.send_state.epmap == post2_self_.send_state.epmap and similarly for receive_state). HashMap<V> in this codebase is declared with #[verifier(external_body)] and exposes only `pub uninterp spec fn view(self) -> Map<...>` with no extensional/structural equality axiom. The send_single_message spec only constrains post-states at the abstract view level (via .view() and the insert on Map), so two arbitrary post-state values that both satisfy the spec can have the same abstract view but be different SMT values at the concrete HashMap level — there's no available lemma to lift view equality to concrete `==`. Attempt 1 used a forall determinism lemma for send_single_message; attempt 2 asserted the epmap equalities directly. Both produced 'postcondition not satisfied' / 'assertion failed'. Without introducing new axioms (forbidden), this proof cannot be closed.
